result:
All three Plan CRDs are well-formed. Here is the complete phase brief and decomposition:

---

## Phase Brief — `phase-02-codex-subagent-core`

**Milestone:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`  
**Depends on:** `phase-01-codex-pricing-usage`

---

### Objective

Build the `internal/subagent/codex/` Go package — the complete, tested `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` implementation for the Codex (OpenAI) runtime — behind the provider firewall. By this phase's exit the package is end-to-end correct and fully offline-testable; the container image (phase-03) and the controller-side vendor switch (phase-04) consume it unchanged.

---

### Scope — six files, all in `internal/subagent/codex/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `doc.go` | Package comment — D-C1 layering reference; `"openai"` vendor sentinel; D6 CLI headless contract |
| `client.go` | `Client` struct, `Options`, `New()`, `NewWithExec()` — execFunc seam for offline test injection |
| `stream_parser.go` | `ParseStream(r, rawSink)` — Codex `--json` JSONL → final message + `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5 |
| `stream_parser_test.go` | Offline: happy path, usage mapping, CacheCreationTokens == 0 invariant, tolerates-non-JSON |
| `run.go` | `Subagent.Run()` — vendor guard, D6 invocation, stream parse, usage/cost, child-CRD handoff, traversal defenses |
| `run_test.go` | Offline via fake `codex` binary: vendor mismatch, fixture JSONL, child-CRD roundtrip, OPENAI_API_KEY guard |

Phase-01's `pricing.go` + `pricing_test.go` are pre-conditions — consumed read-only by `run.go`.

---

### Plan Decomposition (three Plans across two waves)

```
Wave 1 — parallel, no intra-phase deps
  plan-01-codex-scaffold       →  doc.go, client.go
  plan-02-codex-stream-parser  →  stream_parser.go, stream_parser_test.go

Wave 2 — after wave 1 completes
  plan-03-codex-run            →  run.go, run_test.go
                                  (imports Client + ParseStream + phase-01 pricing)
```

---

### Verification Gates

| Gate | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| V1 | `make test ./internal/subagent/codex/...` exits 0, zero network calls |
| V2 | `make verify-import-firewall` green — no codex/OpenAI SDK in controller or cmd/manager |
| V3 | `make verify-dispatch-imports` green — provider firewall intact |
| V4 | `Run()` refuses `Provider.Vendor != "openai"` (fail-fast, unit tested) |
| V5 | `CacheReadTokens ← cached_tokens`; `CacheCreationTokens == 0` always (unit tested) |
| V6 | No real OpenAI API call without `OPENAI_API_KEY` env set |
| V7 | `make test` full suite green — zero regressions |

---

### Child Plan CRDs emitted

Three JSON files have been written to `children/`:

- **`plan-01-codex-scaffold.json`** — wave 1, no deps → `doc.go`, `client.go`
- **`plan-02-codex-stream-parser.json`** — wave 1, no deps → `stream_parser.go`, `stream_parser_test.go`
- **`plan-03-codex-run.json`** — wave 2, depends on plan-01 + plan-02 → `run.go`, `run_test.go`

spec.objective: Author the foundational scaffold for internal/subagent/codex/: doc.go naming the D-C1 layering pattern and the openai vendor sentinel, and client.go declaring the Client struct, Options, New() constructor, and NewWithExec() test-seam — mirroring internal/subagent/anthropic/ exactly. The execFunc field on Client is the indirection seam that lets tests inject a fake codex binary offline. No Run() method lives in this file. No OpenAI SDK imports introduced; the only provider dependency is the codex CLI binary resolved via PATH at runtime.

Author stream_parser.go and stream_parser_test.go for internal/subagent/codex/. ParseStream reads the Codex CLI --json JSONL event stream (one event per line), tees raw bytes to rawSink (the per-task events.jsonl audit log), and extracts the final message plus a pkg/dispatch.Usage struct per D5: usage.prompt_tokens to InputTokens, usage.completion_tokens to OutputTokens, usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens to CacheReadTokens, and CacheCreationTokens fixed at zero (OpenAI caching is automatic and marker-free — no cache-write billing concept exists). Uses common.ReadLines for the 16MB per-line budget and tolerates non-JSON lines silently per the anthropic pattern. Tests are fully offline: happy path, usage-mapping invariant (CacheCreationTokens == 0 always), tolerates-non-JSON, and no-result-event. No dependency on plan-01-codex-scaffold.

Author run.go and run_test.go for internal/subagent/codex/, completing the pkg/dispatch.Subagent implementation. run.go implements Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn): (1) vendor fail-fast guard refusing any envelope whose Provider.Vendor != openai; (2) optional params allow-list for known Codex tuning keys; (3) in-pod prompt artifact read via readPromptArtifact when PromptPath is set; (4) events dir creation and D6 CLI invocation (codex exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --json --sandbox workspace-write --output-schema, with prompt via stdin); (5) OPENAI_API_KEY env wiring from in.SignedToken; (6) JSONL stream parsing via plan-02 ParseStream teed to events.jsonl; (7) Usage population via plan-01 estimatedCostCents from the phase-01 pricing table; (8) child-CRD file handoff for planner role via readChildCRDs (all traversal defenses, kind allowlist, double-object detection, sanitizeJSONStringControls — mirroring anthropic/subagent.go); (9) task-level vs dispatch-level error semantics per pkg/dispatch.Subagent godoc. run_test.go tests are fully offline using the execFunc seam from plan-01 Client and a fake codex binary (bash -c cat pattern): vendor mismatch, fixture JSONL stream end-to-end, child-CRD handoff, conservative-tier fallback for unknown model. Any test path that would invoke a real OpenAI endpoint is guarded by an explicit OPENAI_API_KEY env check and skipped when absent.
